query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 410d8849).

## Issue to Resolve

Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Autoformatting should not reformat below line to incorrect state
```
private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Observed Behavior
Autoformatting moves `DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))` to the next line and as the results it looks like this:
```
    private val deferredMessageQueueSubscription = 
    DeferredMessageQueueSubscription(uri = ServiceBusUri("deferredQueueTestUri"))
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Autoformatting should allow to reproduce this. My Detekt config:
```
formatting:
    excludes: [ '**/build/**' ]
    MaximumLineLength:
        active: false
    NoBlankLineBeforeRbrace:
        active: false
    Indentation:
        active: false
    ArgumentListWrapping:
        active: false
    MultiLineIfElse:
        active: false
```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.49.1
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used: Detekt 1.23.0. Related issue https://github.com/detekt/detekt/issues/6223
* Version of Gradle: 7.3.3
* Kotlin version 1.8.21

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 26→import mu.KotlinLogging
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 28→
 29→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}
172→
173→public val PROPERTY_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyWrappingRule().ruleId
174→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
73→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
74→    }

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 28→import mu.KotlinLogging
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 35→ *
 36→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 37→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 38→ * indentation rule is not run.
 39→ *
 40→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 41→ */
 42→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private var line = 1
 53→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}
180→
181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId
182→

 33→/**
 34→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 35→ *
 36→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 37→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 38→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 39→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 40→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 41→ */
 42→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 45→        visitorModifiers =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case the max_line_length is still violated after running rules below:
 48→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 49→                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 50→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 51→                ),
 52→            ),
 53→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 54→            setOf(
 55→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 56→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 57→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 58→            ),
 59→    ) {
 60→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 61→
 62→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 65→        editorConfigIndent =
 66→            IndentConfig(
 67→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 68→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 69→            )
 70→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 74→        node: ASTNode,
 75→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 76→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 77→    ) {
 78→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 79→            return
 80→        }
 81→
 82→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 83→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 84→                node
 85→                    .children()
 86→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 87→            }
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 92→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 93→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 94→            // skip lambda arguments
 95→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 96→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 97→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8 &&
 98→            // skip if part of a value argument list. It depends on the situation whether it is better to wrap the arguments in the list
 99→            // or the operators in the binary expression
100→            !node.isPartOf(BINARY_EXPRESSION)
101→        ) {
102→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
103→            // - at least one of the arguments is
104→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
105→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
106→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
107→        } else {
108→            false
109→        }
110→
111→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = lineLengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
112→
113→    private fun intendedIndent(child: ASTNode): String =
114→        when {
115→            // IDEA quirk:
116→            // generic<
117→            //     T,
118→            //     R>(
119→            //     1,
120→            //     2
121→            // )
122→            // instead of
123→            // generic<
124→            //     T,
125→            //     R>(
126→            //         1,
127→            //         2
128→            //     )
129→            child.treeParent.hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> -1
130→
131→            // IDEA quirk:
132→            // foo
133→            //     .bar = Baz(
134→            //     1,
135→            //     2
136→            // )
137→            // instead of
138→            // foo
139→            //     .bar = Baz(
140→            //         1,
141→            //         2
142→            //     )
143→            child.treeParent.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> -1
144→
145→            else -> 0
146→        }.let {
147→            if (child.treeParent.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
148→                it + 1
149→            } else {
150→                it
151→            }
152→        }.let {
153→            if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
154→                it + 1
155→            } else {
156→                it
157→            }
158→        }.let { indentLevelFix ->
159→            val indentLevel =
160→                editorConfigIndent
161→                    .indentLevelFrom(child.treeParent.indent(false))
162→                    .plus(indentLevelFix)
163→            "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(indentLevel)
164→        }
165→
166→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
167→        child: ASTNode,
168→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
169→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
170→    ) {
171→        when (child.elementType) {
172→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
173→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
174→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
175→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
176→                    if (autoCorrect) {
177→                        prevLeaf.delete()
178→                    }
179→                }
180→            }
181→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
182→            ElementType.RPAR,
183→            -> {
184→                // aiming for
185→                // ... LPAR
186→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
187→                // <line indent> RPAR
188→                val intendedIndent = intendedIndent(child)
189→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
190→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
191→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
192→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
193→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
194→                        return
195→                    } else {
196→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
197→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
198→                        if (autoCorrect) {
199→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
200→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
201→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
202→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
203→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
204→                        }
205→                    }
206→                } else {
207→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
208→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
209→                    if (autoCorrect) {
210→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
211→                    }
212→                }
213→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
214→            }
215→        }
216→    }
217→
218→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
219→        when (node.elementType) {
220→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
221→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
222→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
223→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
224→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
225→        }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
228→        return children().any { child ->
229→            val elementType = child.elementType
230→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
231→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
232→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
233→        }
234→    }
235→
236→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
237→        treeParent.children()
238→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
239→            ?.children()
240→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
241→
242→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
243→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
244→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
245→
246→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
247→        var prev = prevLeaf()
248→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
249→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
250→                return prev
251→            }
252→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
253→        }
254→        return null
255→    }
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
258→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
259→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
260→        val controlFlowKeyword =
261→            when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
262→                is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
263→                is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
264→                is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
265→                else -> null
266→            } ?: return false
267→
268→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
269→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
270→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
271→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
272→        }
273→        return true
274→    }
275→}
276→
277→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId
278→

  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GT
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PROJECTION
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 31→
 32→/**
 33→ * Wrapping of context receiver to a separate line. Arguments of the context receiver are wrapped to separate line
 34→ * whenever the max line length is exceeded.
 35→ */
 36→public class ContextReceiverWrappingRule :
 37→    StandardRule(
 38→        id = "context-receiver-wrapping",
 39→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 40→            setOf(
 41→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 42→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 43→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 44→            ),
 45→    ),
 46→    Rule.Experimental {
 47→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 48→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 49→
 50→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 51→        indentConfig =
 52→            IndentConfig(
 53→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 54→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 55→            )
 56→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 60→        node: ASTNode,
 61→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 62→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 63→    ) {
 64→        when {
 65→            node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ->
 66→                visitContextReceiverList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 67→            node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST && node.isPartOf(CONTEXT_RECEIVER) ->
 68→                visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 69→        }
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    private fun visitContextReceiverList(
 73→        node: ASTNode,
 74→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 75→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 76→    ) {
 77→        // Context receiver must be followed by new line or comment
 78→        node
 79→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 80→            .nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 81→            ?.takeIf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
 82→            ?.let { nodeAfterContextReceiver ->
 83→                emit(nodeAfterContextReceiver.startOffset, "Expected a newline after the context receiver", true)
 84→                if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                    nodeAfterContextReceiver
 86→                        .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→                        .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
 88→                }
 89→            }
 90→
 91→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run before indenting.
 92→        if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
 93→            node.indent(false).length + node.textLength > maxLineLength
 94→        ) {
 95→            node
 96→                .children()
 97→                .filter { it.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER }
 98→                .forEach {
 99→                    emit(
100→                        it.startOffset,
101→                        "Newline expected before context receiver as max line length is violated",
102→                        true,
103→                    )
104→                    if (autoCorrect) {
105→                        it
106→                            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
107→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
108→                    }
109→                }
110→            node
111→                .findChildByType(RPAR)
112→                ?.let { rpar ->
113→                    emit(
114→                        rpar.startOffset,
115→                        "Newline expected before closing parenthesis as max line length is violated",
116→                        true,
117→                    )
118→                    if (autoCorrect) {
119→                        rpar.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
120→                    }
121→                }
122→        }
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun visitContextReceiverTypeArgumentList(
126→        node: ASTNode,
127→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
128→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129→    ) {
130→        val contextReceiver = node.treeParent.text
131→        // Check line length assuming that the context receiver is indented correctly. Wrapping rule must however run
132→        // before indenting.
133→        if (!contextReceiver.contains('\n') &&
134→            node.indent(false).length + contextReceiver.length > maxLineLength
135→        ) {
136→            node
137→                .children()
138→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
139→                .forEach {
140→                    emit(
141→                        it.startOffset,
142→                        "Newline expected before context receiver type projection as max line length is violated",
143→                        true,
144→                    )
145→                    if (autoCorrect) {
146→                        it.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
147→                    }
148→                }
149→            node
150→                .findChildByType(GT)
151→                ?.let { gt ->
152→                    emit(
153→                        gt.startOffset,
154→                        "Newline expected before closing angle bracket as max line length is violated",
155→                        true,
156→                    )
157→                    if (autoCorrect) {
158→                        // Ideally, the closing angle bracket should be de-indented to make it consistent with
159→                        // de-intentation of closing ")", "}" and "]". This however would be inconsistent with how the
160→                        // type argument lists are formatted by IntelliJ IDEA default formatter.
161→                        gt.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
162→                    }
163→                }
164→        }
165→    }
166→}
167→
168→public val CONTEXT_RECEIVER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ContextReceiverWrappingRule().ruleId
169→

  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY

 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtClass
 27→
 28→/**
 29→ *
 30→ */
 31→public class EnumWrappingRule :
 32→    StandardRule(
 33→        id = "enum-wrapping",
 34→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 35→            setOf(
 36→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 37→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 38→            ),
 39→    ),
 40→    Rule.Experimental {
 41→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 42→
 43→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 44→        indentConfig =
 45→            IndentConfig(
 46→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 47→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 48→            )
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 52→        node: ASTNode,
 53→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 54→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 55→    ) {
 56→        node
 57→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS }
 58→            ?.takeIf { (node.psi as KtClass).isEnum() }
 59→            ?.findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)
 60→            ?.let { classBody ->
 61→                visitEnumClass(classBody, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun visitEnumClass(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        require(node.elementType == CLASS_BODY)
 71→
 72→        val commentBeforeFirstEnumEntry = wrapCommentBeforeFirstEnumEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 73→        if (commentBeforeFirstEnumEntry || node.isMultiline() || node.hasAnnotatedEnumEntry() || node.hasCommentedEnumEntry()) {
 74→            wrapEnumEntries(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 75→            wrapClosingBrace(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→        addBlankLineBetweenEnumEntriesAndOtherDeclarations(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    private fun wrapCommentBeforeFirstEnumEntry(
 81→        node: ASTNode,
 82→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 83→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 84→    ): Boolean {
 85→        val firstEnumEntry = node.findChildByType(ENUM_ENTRY)
 86→        if (firstEnumEntry != null) {
 87→            node
 88→                .children()
 89→                .takeWhile { it != firstEnumEntry }
 90→                .firstOrNull { it.isPartOfComment() }
 91→                ?.let { commentBeforeFirstEnumEntry ->
 92→                    val expectedIndent = indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node)
 93→                    if (commentBeforeFirstEnumEntry.prevLeaf()?.text != expectedIndent) {
 94→                        emit(node.startOffset, "Expected a newline before comment", true)
 95→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 96→                            commentBeforeFirstEnumEntry.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
 97→                        }
 98→                        return true
 99→                    }
100→                }
101→        }
102→        return false
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.isMultiline() = text.contains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.hasAnnotatedEnumEntry() =
108→        children()
109→            .filter { it.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY }
110→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated(): Boolean =
113→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
114→            ?.children()
115→            .orEmpty()
116→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.hasCommentedEnumEntry() = children().any { it.containsCommentInEnumEntry() }
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.containsCommentInEnumEntry() = children().any { it.isPartOfComment() }
121→
122→    private fun wrapEnumEntries(
123→        node: ASTNode,
124→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→    ) {
127→        node
128→            .children()
129→            .filter { it.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY }
130→            .forEach { enumEntry ->
131→                wrapEnumEntry(enumEntry, autoCorrect, emit)
132→            }
133→    }
134→
135→    private fun wrapEnumEntry(
136→        node: ASTNode,
137→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
138→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
139→    ) {
140→        node
141→            .prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
142→            ?.takeUnless { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
143→            ?.let { prevLeaf ->
144→                emit(node.startOffset, "Enum entry should start on a separate line", true)
145→                if (autoCorrect) {
146→                    prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
147→                }
148→            }
149→    }
150→
151→    private fun wrapClosingBrace(
152→        node: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        node
157→            .findChildByType(RBRACE)
158→            ?.let { rbrace ->
159→                val prevLeaf = rbrace.prevLeaf()
160→                val expectedIndent = indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node)
161→                if (prevLeaf?.text != expectedIndent) {
162→                    emit(rbrace.startOffset, "Expected newline before '}'", true)
163→                    if (autoCorrect) {
164→                        rbrace.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
165→                    }
166→                }
167→            }
168→    }
169→
170→    private fun addBlankLineBetweenEnumEntriesAndOtherDeclarations(
171→        node: ASTNode,
172→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
173→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
174→    ) {
175→        node
176→            .children()
177→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY }
178→            ?.nextSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
179→            ?.takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == RBRACE }
180→            ?.let { nextSibling ->
181→                val expectedIndent = "\n".plus(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
182→                if (nextSibling.text != expectedIndent) {
183→                    emit(nextSibling.startOffset + 1, "Expected blank line between enum entries and other declaration(s)", true)
184→                    if (autoCorrect) {
185→                        nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
186→                    }
187→                }
188→            }
189→    }
190→}
191→
192→public val ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = EnumWrappingRule().ruleId
193→

 35→/**
 36→ * Enforce that single line if statements are kept simple. A single line if statement is allowed only when it has at most one else branch.
 37→ * Also, the branches of such an if statement may not be wrapped in a block.
 38→ */
 39→public class IfElseWrappingRule :
 40→    StandardRule(
 41→        id = "if-else-wrapping",
 42→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED)),
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→            ),
 48→    ),
 49→    Rule.Experimental,
 50→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
 51→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→
 53→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 54→        indentConfig =
 55→            IndentConfig(
 56→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 57→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 58→            )
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 62→        node: ASTNode,
 63→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→    ) {
 66→        if (node.elementType == IF) {
 67→            visitIf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun visitIf(
 72→        node: ASTNode,
 73→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 74→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 75→    ) {
 76→        val outerIf = node.outerIf()
 77→        val multilineIf = outerIf.textContains('\n')
 78→        val nestedIf = outerIf.isNestedIf()
 79→        with(node) {
 80→            findChildByType(THEN)
 81→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 82→            findChildByType(ELSE_KEYWORD)
 83→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 84→            findChildByType(ELSE)
 85→                ?.let { visitElement(it, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf, nestedIf) }
 86→        }
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    private fun visitElement(
 90→        node: ASTNode,
 91→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93→        multilineIf: Boolean,
 94→        nestedIf: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        if (!multilineIf) {
 97→            visitBranchSingleLineIf(node, emit)
 98→        }
 99→        if (multilineIf || nestedIf) {
100→            visitBranch(node, autoCorrect, emit, multilineIf)
101→        }
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun visitBranchSingleLineIf(
105→        node: ASTNode,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        node
109→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
110→            ?.let {
111→                // The then or else block is part of single line if-statement. To enforce that such statements are readable and
112→                // simple, blocks are forbidden entirely in single line if-statements.
113→                emit(
114→                    node.startOffset,
115→                    "A single line if-statement should be kept simple. The '${node.elementType}' may not be wrapped in a block.",
116→                    false,
117→                )
118→            }
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun visitBranch(
122→        node: ASTNode,
123→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
124→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
125→        multilineIf: Boolean,
126→    ) {
127→        if (multilineIf) {
128→            if (node.elementType == IF && node.prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
129→                node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD ||
130→                node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD
131→            ) {
132→                // Allow "else if" on single line
133→                return
134→            }
135→            if (node.elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE) {
136→                // Allow "else {" on single line
137→                return
138→            }
139→        } else {
140→            // Outer if statement is a single line statement
141→            if (node.elementType == ELSE && node.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType == IF_KEYWORD) {
142→                // Ignore "else if" as it is reported via another message
143→                return
144→            }
145→        }
146→
147→        with(node.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented() ?: node) {
148→            val expectedIndent =
149→                if (nextSibling()?.elementType == RBRACE) {
150→                    node.indent()
151→                } else {
152→                    indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node)
153→                }
154→
155→            applyIf(elementType == THEN || elementType == ELSE || elementType == ELSE_KEYWORD) { prevLeaf()!! }
156→                .takeUnless { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
157→                ?.let {
158→                    // Expected a newline with indent. Leave it up to the IndentationRule to determine exact indent
159→                    emit(startOffset, "Expected a newline", true)
160→                    if (autoCorrect) {
161→                        upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedIndent)
162→                    }
163→                }
164→        }
165→    }
166→
167→//         private fun ASTNode.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented(): ASTNode? {
168→//            val blockOrSelf = findChildByType(BLOCK) ?: this
169→//            return blockOrSelf
170→//                .children()
171→//                .find {
172→//                    it.elementType != LBRACE &&
173→//                        !it.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() &&
174→//                        !it.isPartOfComment()
175→//                }
176→//        }
177→    private fun ASTNode.findFirstNodeInBlockToBeIndented(): ASTNode? {
178→        return findChildByType(BLOCK)
179→            ?.children()
180→            ?.first {
181→                it.elementType != LBRACE &&
182→                    !it.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() &&
183→                    !it.isPartOfComment()
184→            }
185→    }
186→
187→    private fun ASTNode.isWhitespaceBeforeComment() = isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() && nextLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true
188→
189→    private fun ASTNode.outerIf(): ASTNode {
190→        require(this.elementType == IF)
191→        return parents()
192→            .takeWhile { it.elementType in IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES }
193→            .lastOrNull()
194→            ?: this
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun ASTNode.isNestedIf(): Boolean {
198→        require(elementType == IF)
199→        return findChildByType(THEN)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF ||
200→            findChildByType(ELSE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF
201→    }
202→
203→    private companion object {
204→        val IF_THEN_ELSE_ELEMENT_TYPES =
205→            listOf(
206→                IF,
207→                THEN,
208→                ELSE,
209→            )
210→    }
211→}
212→
213→public val IF_ELSE_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = IfElseWrappingRule().ruleId
214→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
  8→
  9→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 10→
 11→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 12→
 13→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 14→
 15→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 16→
 17→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 18→
 19→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 20→
 21→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 22→
 23→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 24→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 25→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 26→
 27→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 28→
 29→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 30→
 31→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 32→
 33→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 34→
 35→### Added
 36→
 37→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 38→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 39→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 40→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 41→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 42→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 43→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 44→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 45→
 46→### Removed
 47→
 48→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 49→
 50→### Fixed
 51→
 52→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 53→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 54→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 55→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 56→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 57→
 58→### Changed
 59→
 60→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 61→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 62→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 63→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 64→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 65→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 66→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 67→
 68→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 69→
 70→### Added
 71→
 72→### Removed
 73→
 74→### Fixed
 75→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 76→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 77→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 78→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 79→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 80→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 81→* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
 82→* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 83→* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
 84→* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
 85→* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
 86→* Prevent multiple expressions on same line separated by semicolon ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 87→
 88→### Changed
 89→
 90→* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
 91→
 92→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 93→
 94→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 95→
 96→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 97→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 98→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 99→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
100→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
